stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (Apr 21, 2026): PAT +19.15% YoY in Q4 and FY26 PAT +18% YoY to Rs 2,291 cr. Retail loan book +16% YoY to Rs 86,946 cr; total loan book Rs 87,347 cr. Q4 disbursements +36% YoY to Rs 9,355 cr. NIM +6 bps QoQ to 3.69%. Gross NPA improved to 0.93%. ROA 2.66%, ROE 12.73%. Final dividend Rs 8/share recommended. Stock at Rs 1,065.60 on May 6, +2.23% intraday, +30.28% over the past month.
stocks.sector_context: Housing finance NBFCs benefit from RBI's 125 bps cumulative repo cuts through FY25 with easing bias maintained into FY26. Affordable housing segment growing 22-23%. NBFCs growing fastest in segment (~18% CAGR projected through 2031). Lower funding costs improving lending spreads.
stocks.peer_comparison: Best-in-class GNPA at 0.93% (sub-1%) versus typical HFC peer range. Disbursement growth +36% YoY meaningfully outpaces sector. Stock outperformed sector with +30% one-month return. NIM expansion bucks sector compression trend.
red_flags: Stock has rallied ~30% in past month - post-results upside largely priced in
RBI's draft scale-based regulations could tighten oversight on upper-layer NBFCs
Near-term consolidation risk after vertical move
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 8/share at upcoming AGM (subject to shareholder approval)
Continued retail loan book momentum (+16% YoY)
Potential further RBI rate cuts in FY27

stocks.recent_news: Trading at Rs 124.27 on May 7, 2026 (+0.20%). NAV Rs 123.43 as of Apr 30, 2026. AUM at Rs 54,127.54 cr. 52-week range Rs 76.36 - Rs 148.14. Domestic 24K gold at Rs 1,49,500-1,52,000 per 10g; 22K at Rs 1,37,000-1,40,000. Indian gold market exhibiting stable, slightly bullish trend driven by wedding-season demand and rupee weakness.
stocks.sector_context: Gold supported by structural tailwinds: continued central bank buying, geopolitical uncertainty, de-dollarization theme, weak INR raising imported gold cost. Some analysts project gold could test Rs 2 lakh per 10g by end-2026 if currency weakness persists.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest gold ETF in India by AUM (Rs 54,127 cr), high liquidity vs peer gold ETFs from HDFC/ICICI/SBI. Tracks domestic spot gold closely with 0.80% expense ratio.
red_flags: Currently ~16% off 52-week high of Rs 148.14 - in consolidation phase
0.80% expense ratio is on the higher side vs newer gold ETF peers
Gold often sees pullbacks after parabolic moves; near-term mean reversion risk
catalysts: Geopolitical tension flare-ups driving safe-haven flows

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results: Revenue +26.5% YoY to Rs 10,737.58 cr; PAT +9.4% YoY to Rs 3,308.30 cr; EBITDA +20.3% YoY to Rs 6,020 cr; EBITDA margin ~56%. International ports revenue surged to Rs 1,422 cr (from Rs 901 cr) with EBITDA Rs 597 cr (from Rs 131 cr) led by NQXT Australia and CWIT Colombo. Dividend Rs 7.50/share with record date Jun 12, 2026. Stock up ~25% in the month preceding results. Promoter entity sold 2% stake for Rs 7,486 cr in early May 2026. Targeting Rs 13,000 cr Europe Marine investment.
stocks.sector_context: Indian port/logistics sector benefiting from global trade rebalancing and India's manufacturing push. Strong cargo throughput growth across major ports. International port acquisitions providing geographic diversification beyond India risk.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest private port operator in India; international expansion (Australia, Sri Lanka) differentiates from JNPT/domestic peers. EBITDA margins at ~56% remain industry-leading. Logistics segment growing slower than ports.
red_flags: Promoter 2% stake sale (~Rs 7,486 cr) in early May 2026 - mild overhang
Q4 PAT growth (9.4%) lagged revenue growth (26.5%) - some margin pressure visible
Adani group governance/Hindenburg legacy continues to attract scrutiny
Historical pledged-shares concerns persist for the group
catalysts: Rs 13,000 cr Europe marine market expansion
Continued NQXT Australia and CWIT Colombo ramp-up

stocks.recent_news: Stock soared 6% to Rs 4,578.90 in Monday May 4 intraday trade on heavy volumes; +31% since April 2026. New CMD Kota Ravi (formerly Director Operations) assumed charge May 1, 2026 as 22nd Chairman; track record on 180 LCA Tejas and 156 LCH Prachand orders. Order book at Rs 2.54 trillion. Active execution on 156 LCH for Army/IAF, 240 AL-31FP engines for Su-30MKI fleet, 12 Su-30MKI aircraft, Dornier-228 Mid-Life Upgrade. Analysts project 50%+ manufacturing-revenue CAGR for FY26-FY28.
stocks.sector_context: Indian defense PSU sector saw post-Budget 2026 correction on stretched valuations - HAL, BEL, MDL, BDL, GRSE all sold off after rally. Sector transitioning from expectation-driven to execution-driven phase. Structural Indian defense indigenization story intact, but near-term valuations elevated.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest defense PSU by order book (Rs 2.54 tn) vs BEL, BDL, Mazagon Dock. Trading at 30x+ P/E - well above historical average and in line with defense PSU peer premium. New CMD continuity reduces leadership risk vs peers undergoing transitions.
red_flags: P/E above 30x - significantly stretched vs historical averages
Sector-wide post-Budget correction signals valuation fatigue
Execution risk inherent to PSU defense manufacturing
Geopolitical normalization could reduce urgency premium
catalysts: LCA Tejas Mk1A delivery ramp-up (key near-term execution catalyst)
New CMD Kota Ravi mandate and strategic announcements
Fresh defense order awards from MoD

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (May 5, 2026): Revenue +34% YoY to Rs 938 cr; EBITDA +80% YoY to Rs 321 cr (margin 34.2%); PAT +124% YoY to Rs 213 cr. Performance driven by CDMO ramp, operational improvements, and favorable refrigerant gas pricing. Stock hit intraday high Rs 7,010 on May 5 and lifetime high Rs 7,200 the day after; +51% since end-Sep 2025. Geojit projected target Rs 8,500 (~21% upside) on bullish technical setup.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals sector facing H1 FY26 headwinds from US tariffs (R32/R125, MMA, 2,4-D) impacting SRF, Navin Fluorine, GFL, Aarti. However, fluorochemicals remain a structurally advantaged segment with limited global competition. China+1 supply diversification and pharma/agrochem CDMO demand continue to support medium-term growth.
stocks.peer_comparison: Trading at premium P/E (50x-70x) vs SRF/GFL/Aarti - significant valuation gap. EBITDA margin 34.2% best-in-class among Indian fluorochemical peers. CDMO mix shift differentiates vs commodity-heavy peers. Q4 PAT growth (+124%) and EBITDA growth (+80%) materially outpace SRF/Aarti.
red_flags: P/E of 50x-70x is significantly elevated even for premium specialty chemicals peer set
US tariffs on key refrigerant exports (R32/R125) a direct headwind
Margin sustainability questioned given competition and input-cost volatility
Stock at lifetime highs - exhibit asymmetric near-term downside on any disappointment
catalysts: Continued CDMO contract wins and ramp-up
Geojit technical target Rs 8,500 (potential momentum target)
Capex commissioning and new MPP capacity ramp
